instruction: Let the other person say the word first - then check it here.
emptyBody: Create a shared codeword for a person or group. Anyone who enters the same passphrase sees the same word.
addNote: The passphrase itself is not stored - only a key derived from it. The name stays on this device.
errCrypto: This device can't use the required encryption. Please open the page over HTTPS.
errAdd: Couldn't add it. Please try again.
httpsSub: Please open the page over HTTPS - otherwise the encryption won't work.
switchLang: Current language: English. Switch to German.
pwShort: Short - a longer passphrase is safer.
hintPh: e.g. the name of our cat
hintNote: Make it something only you would understand — not obvious to a stranger.
lostPassTitle: Did they say they lost access to their app?
lostPassNoPassNeeded: That's fine — they just need to re-add the codeword on their device. They can open this same website or app, tap "Add codeword", and enter the shared passphrase again. No need to share anything with them.
lostPassForgotTitle: Do they also say they forgot the passphrase?
lostPassBody: That is more unusual. Be careful. A real family member or friend should know the passphrase.
lostPassHowTo: If you still want to help: tap the "Show hint" button on the codeword card below. Read only the hint out loud — nothing else.
lostPassWarning: Do not share the passphrase itself.
lostPassUncertain: If you feel uncertain at any point, get another family member involved.
lostPassUrgency: They might say it's super urgent - it's always worth the 2 minutes to involve another person.
